findings.summary: FetchHttpClient and HttpSupportResistanceSource implement immediate retry loops without any backoff delay, which can exacerbate server overload and rate limits.
findings.suggested_fix: Inject RetryControl (e.g. SystemRetryControl) or add an await sleep() backoff delay before retrying.

findings.summary: FetchHttpClient.postJsonRaw returns the raw string body cast as type T when JSON parsing fails, violating type safety and potentially causing downstream runtime errors.
findings.suggested_fix: Throw an HttpRequestError("invalid_json", ...) when JSON.parse fails, matching the behavior of getJson.

findings.summary: HttpSupportResistanceSource throws a generic Error for unsupported pairs instead of a domain-specific SupportResistanceSourceError.
findings.suggested_fix: Throw mapToSupportResistanceSourceError(new HttpRequestError(...)) or a direct SupportResistanceSourceError for unsupported pairs.

findings.summary: Spreading options.headers over default headers in postJsonRaw can result in duplicate case-variant headers (e.g., 'Content-Type' and 'content-type').
findings.suggested_fix: Use a Headers object to merge headers case-insensitively before passing to fetchOptions.
